# Task: Migrate a Git Repository to Jujutsu

You are a developer migrating a project from Git to Jujutsu (jj). The repository is located at `/home/user/project` and is currently a standard Git repository. There is also a bare Git repository at `/home/user/upstream.git` that serves as the upstream remote.

You need to:
1. Initialize jj in the existing Git repository to enable co-location.
2. Add a new Git remote named `upstream` pointing to `/home/user/upstream.git`.
3. Fetch from the `upstream` remote.
4. Rebase your current working copy onto the `main@upstream` branch.

todos.content: Inspect repository state and verify Git/Jujutsu setup
Initialize jj in the existing Git repository for co-location
Add Git remote named upstream pointing to /home/user/upstream.git
Fetch from upstream remote via jj
Rebase current working copy onto main@upstream

arguments.command: git status --short --branch && git remote -v || true && jj --version

todos.content: Inspect repository state and verify Git/Jujutsu setup
Initialize jj in the existing Git repository for co-location
Add Git remote named upstream pointing to /home/user/upstream.git
Fetch from upstream remote via jj
Rebase current working copy onto main@upstream

arguments.result:
Completed the Git→Jujutsu migration steps in `/home/user/project`.

Actions performed:
- Initialized Jujutsu co-location with `jj git init --colocate`
- Added Git remote `upstream` pointing to `/home/user/upstream.git`
- Fetched from that remote with `jj git fetch --remote upstream`
- Rebased the working copy onto `main@upstream` with `jj rebase --source @ --destination main@upstream`

Result:
- `main@upstream` is now available locally
- The working copy is rebased on top of `main@upstream`
- Current parent commit is `ba4e10fd` on `main@upstream`

For help or feedback, join the Discord: https://getpochi.com/discord
To report bugs, use: https://github.com/TabbyML/pochi/issues
